subject: Physics
title: Galileo's Ship of Relativity
description: A quick, practical guide to Galileo's Ship of Relativity.

hook.text: Galileo imagined being below deck on a smooth-sailing ship. No experiment could tell if the ship was moving or still.

buildup.text: Butterflies fly normally. Water drips straight down. A ball thrown any direction behaves the same.

discovery.text: Galileo realized that the laws of physics are the same in any uniformly moving reference frame.

twist.text: This principle of relativity—from 1632—is the direct ancestor of Einstein's special relativity 273 years later.

climax.text: It means there is no absolute rest or absolute motion. Only relative motion between objects matters.

punchline.text: No experiment detects smooth motion. Same physics everywhere. Galileo got there first.

quiz.question: What did Galileo's ship thought experiment establish?
options: That the laws of physics are the same in any uniformly moving frame
That ships move faster than carriages
